name: Bill Coleman
abstract: William Johnson Coleman (August 4, 1904 in Paris, Kentucky, United States – August 24, 1981 in Toulouse, France) was an American jazz trumpeter.

quotes.text: World war number two had been going on for nearly two years in America. The trio was beginning to become popular, and we co-starred with Hazel Scott after her return from Hollywood for a benefit to buy watches for the Soviet Army, which had taken place at Carnegie Hall the 11th of April, 1943. The trio did a few numbers and for the finale, we were joined by a trombonist named Frank Orchard, and the trumpetist Jack Butler.
quotes.caption: 1943 benefit concert with Hazel Scott and Frank Orchard

quotes.text: Django came to the States to do some concerts with Duke Ellington, and was to appear in one at Carnegie Hall. We had an hour intermission and there was a small group whose leader was Claude Hopkins, that included Greely Walton, who played the intermission, and after the last show; Greely and I had worked together in Luis Russell's orchestra.
quotes.caption: Django Reinhardt's planned Carnegie Hall appearance with Duke Ellington

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well then he says you went with after that you went with Bill Coleman, toured with Bill Coleman? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes, that's right.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Dillard trumpet, Bill Coleman trumpet. ... The rhythm section was Ernest Hill, Lawrence Lucie, guitar, Teddy Wilson and me.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: And I was playing with Bill Coleman and I'd make that trip almost every week from Paris.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: they had Harold Baker, Big Jim Lawson and myself, and Bill Coleman...
